archive.title: The Constituency Archive
archive.subtitle: Pre-2008 delimitation maps of India's state legislative assemblies

states.name: Arunachal Pradesh
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Assam
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Jammu & Kashmir
states.note: Delimited separately; a 2022 order under the Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act now applies.

states.name: Jharkhand
states.note: Excluded from the 2002-Act exercise by the Delimitation (Amendment) Act, 2008.

states.name: Manipur
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Nagaland
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Telangana
states.note: Constituted in 2014; its pre-2008 constituencies fall under undivided Andhra Pradesh.

homepage.title: The assembly maps India retired in 2008
homepage.lede: In 2008, the boundaries of assembly constituencies across most of India were redrawn. The maps they replaced — on which every state election of the preceding three decades had been fought — have no consolidated public source. Boundaries publishes them, one state at a time.
about_body: The boundaries in force before 2008 derive, in most states, from the orders made under the Delimitation Act, 1972. The Delimitation Act, 2002 constituted a fresh commission working to the 2001 Census, and its orders took effect from 2008. The old maps went out of print with them.
Election archives, court records and constituency histories still refer to the retired constituencies constantly. This site exists so those references can be checked against the actual map.
